feedback.text: I am here to add my pitch for the Stop Killing Games Initiative as it's goals are adjecant to the Digital Fairness Act. I am in all support for the Digital Fairness Act. Stop Killing Games is about making sure that art, in the form of video games, does not collect dust in a storage room, but is instead available to run by anyone with the time, passion and resources. It is not a demand for the creators of video games to continuesly run them, forcing them to spend their resources on video games forever, but for the creators of video games to open up games that are no longer supported by them to the public. Personaly, i would hate for many of the video games i currently play to disappear forever. Such as Team Fight Tactics or Planetside 2. Both games are online only but have a dedicated fanbase. Each has its upsides and downsides but are very loved for what the provide in terms of gameplay, social aspects and art. There are many old games that i am happy are still supported by dedicated fans. Such as Giant Citizen Kabuto. It was a game from my childhood that i only played offline due to my inability to access the internet but hearing that i could visit it's multiplayer, despite never having played it, made me extremely happy. I could experience what i never could before, despite the developers of said video game having abandoned it, leaving fans of it to create small but dedicated servers for multiplayer. When developers abandon online games and never allow them to be run by anyone with an interest, it effectively ends art for no reason. I understand that there is the issue of having a few bad actors try to resell the game or make money off of running the servers for discontinued games but it is going to be a small issue that can be easily taken care of through copyright laws when their actions become large enough to notice. I hope that Stop Killing Games will be implemented in one form or another for the sake of consumer rights.
